items.description: The US president said it's a "waste of time" dealing with Tehran after the US struck dozens of Iranian targets overnight and again later on Wednesday.

items.title: Trump says he believes the Iran ceasefire is 'over.' And, IOC lifts Russia's suspension
items.description: Trump said that he believes the U.S.-Iran ceasefire is over, but hasn't ruled out talks. And, the International Olympic Committee paves the way for Russian athletes to compete in the 2028 games.

items.title: The darkside of 10-minute delivery: Inside India's quick commerce revolution
items.description: India’s rapid-delivery apps have transformed everyday life in its biggest cities, promising almost anything at your doorstep in 10 minutes or less. But behind the convenience lies a fast-growing gig economy that is reshaping jobs, wages and expectations for millions of young Indians. Supriya Kumar and Sacha Desmaizières report.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
items.description: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

items.title: Trump says he will remove Syria from the state sponsor of terrorism list
items.description: US President Trump said he will remove Syria from the US state sponsor of terrorism list at the NATO summit.

items.title: Germany reports 'shock' rise in drug deaths among under-30s
items.description: The number of drug-related deaths remains at a record high in Germany. And the victims are getting younger and younger. Many deaths are related to new and increasingly dangerous substances such as synthetic opioids.

items.title: US court rules that Trump’s name must stay off Kennedy Center during appeal
items.description: Trump’s name was removed from the centre's facade and signage last month, after a judge ordered its removal.

items.title: Rubrik unveils $500 million UK investment, names London as European HQ
Court told former Wagga Wagga MP was 'kept in dark' over visa scheme
items.description: Daryl Maguire's barrister has told a jury it was "unbelievable" to think the ex NSW MP would recruit people in his electorate to take part in an allegedly fraudulent visa scheme.

items.title: Japan bond market signals waning faith in inflation, government's fiscal management
Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
items.description: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.

items.title: Democrat Graham Platner suspends campaign for key US Senate race after assault allegation
items.description: The decision came days after a woman accused him of sexual assault, an accusation he says is "categorically false".

items.title: Fears of a massacre in this city on the front line of Sudan's war
items.description: El-Obeid has been under "siege-like conditions" for 18 months says the UN human rights chief.

items.title: Argentina unveils US$1.2-billion nuclear reactor plan amid CNEA lay-offs row
items.description: US-Argentine firm Meitner Energy will invest US$1.2 billion in a 300-megawatt modular reactor at the Atucha site, the government announced on Tuesday.; News comes as the National Atomic Energy Commission faces a labour dispute over dozens of lay-offs. Leer más

items.title: U.S. launches new strikes on Iran after Trump vows to hit 'hard'
items.description: Both the United States and Iran said they had hit dozens of targets in the initial wave of attacks

items.title: Ex-navy commander convicted over 2017 ARA San Juan submarine tragedy
items.description: Submarine force commander Claudio Villamide handed three-year suspended sentence for negligence and breach of duties; Three other Navy officers acquitted. Leer más

items.title: Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: Austrian court convicts 2 former Syrian officials of abuse
items.description: A Vienna court has convicted two former Syrian security officials, including the former intelligence chief in Raqqa, for offenses including torture and sexual assault against opponents of Bashar Assad's regime.

items.title: Central Bank survey sees inflation dipping below 2% from August
items.description: REM market expectations survey of consultancy firms, economists and experts forecasts 2026 annual inflation of 30%, with peso trading at 1,673 per US dollar by year-end. Leer más

items.title: Dangote-backed mega oil refinery to be built in Kenya
items.description: A 700,000-bpd east African oil refinery proposed by Africa's richest man, Aliko Dangote, will be built in Kenya, a senior company official said Tuesday, putting a lid on speculation over the location of the mega-project.

items.title: Lionel Scaloni: 'I became a coach so I could experience these emotions'
items.description: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni hails his side's character after Tuesday's dramatic 3-2 comeback victory over Egypt. Leer más

items.title: At least five children die in monsoon landslide at a Rohingya camp in Bangladesh
items.description: More than 1 million refugees live in the camp; dollar Tripura, the local chief of the Fire Service and Civil Defence, said the landslide hit as children were attending classes

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Health workers in DR Congo's Ebola outbreak go on strike over pay issues
items.description: The healthcare workers at the epicentre of Congo’s Ebola outbreak are walking off their jobs to protest delays in their payments.

items.title: How undercover agents and informants helped the FBI unravel Lawrence Bishnoi’s gang | Explained
items.description: A years-long U.S. investigation culminated in charges against 37 people linked to alleged transnational crime syndicates. Here’s how the FBI pieced together the case

items.title: Trump's Board of Peace planning pilot humanitarian zone in Gaza, official says
items.description: On Monday (July 6, 2026), Hamas ⁠said it had dissolved its de facto government in Gaza and signalled it was ready to hand over to NCAG, as it presses Israel to honour other parts ‌of the stalled peace plan

items.title: Watch: Why is there a 'black cloud' over unity at the Nato summit?
items.description: US President Donald Trump has used the Nato summit to criticise the lack of support for his war on Iran, and revive his interest in taking over Greenland.

items.title: France's far right set to improve ties with Germany
items.description: France’s right-wing populist Marine Le Pen is running for President. Her chances aren’t bad. Her party, the RN, is already reaching out to Germany.

items.title: US states could sue next week to block Paramount-Warner Bros deal, sources say
🔴Live: US military carrying out fresh strikes against Iran after Trump says peace deal is ‘over’
Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. Leer más

items.title: Ukraine: Suspect in Monaco bomb attack found dead near Kyiv
items.description: Ukrainain prosecutors say the body of the woman suspected of the bombing that targeted businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was found near Kyiv. Police said they had detained two individuals suspected of murdering her.

items.title: Messi World Cup magic masks familiar penalty frailty
items.description: Argentina's captain has a 77% overall career conversion rate from 150 attempts from the spot, bang on the global average. What stands out is such a frailty for a player who is anything but average. Leer más

items.title: India, Australia unveil new defence declaration to deepen Indo-Pacific cooperation
items.description: The agreement also plans for deeper personnel exchanges through education and training while exploring opportunities to cooperate in recruiting skilled personnel for their respective defence workforces. cooperation in recruiting skilled defence personnel

items.title: Strait of Hormuz tanker attack raises tensions as Iran mourns Khamenei
items.description: A liquefied natural gas tanker caught fire after being struck by a projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions in one of the world's most critical shipping lanes. The attack comes as Iran mourns Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and nuclear talks with the United States remain stalled.

items.title: Former Ivory Coast defence minister released after 'terrorism' charges
items.description: Former Ivory Coast Defence Minister Moise Lida Kouassi has been released after spending nearly a year in detention on terrorism-related charges.

items.title: Photos: Israeli attacks kill nine people, including two children, in Gaza
items.description: Israeli air attacks and gunfire kill nine Palestinians in Gaza despite ceasefire, medics say.

items.title: Trump lashes out at NATO allies for not supporting the US war in Iran
items.description: At the alliance summit, he also renewed claims on Greenland and demanded Washington cut trade ties with Spain.

items.title: US says carrying out more strikes after Iran’s attacks in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: The US carried out another round of strikes on Iran on Wednesday, hours after President Donald Trump said that recent Iranian attacks on ships in Strait of Hormuz signalled the end of the ceasefire. Military officials said in a social media post that the strikes were intended to "further degrade" Iran's ability “to threaten freedom of navigation” in the strait. FRANCE 24's Yinka Oyetade speaks with Negah Angha, Visiting Fellow with King's College London.

items.title: Watch: Gaza football fans watch World Cup from rubble
items.description: Hundreds of people cheered for Egypt, which shares a border with Gaza, in the match against Argentina.

items.title: Starmer gifted gun and ammunition by Turkish president at Nato
items.description: The weapon has not been brought back to the UK and has instead been left with British officials in Turkey.

items.title: Cabinets and wardrobes
items.description: Appointment of Diego Santilli places the relations of the ruling La Libertad Avanza party with its chief ally on a new footing. Leer más

items.title: 'We're here to win it again' – Enzo Fernández after Argentina's win over Egypt
items.description: Argentina head coach Lionel Scaloni was unable to speak to the media, overcome with emotion and in tears, after thrilling comeback against Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Nissan management shake-up plans reported as sharks circle
items.description: Nissan is considering a change in leadership following poor results and the failure of a proposed merger with Honda, according to a report citing unnamed sources.

items.title: Argentina targets investment grade rating by 2031, Caputo says
items.description: Milei administration is targeting investment-grade status for Argentina by the end of a potential second term in 2031, announces minister. Leer más

items.title: The IOC clears the way for Russian athletes to return to Olympic competition
items.description: The International Olympic Committee decision effectively overturns a decade-long ban on Russian athletes from international sport imposed due to a state-sponsored doping scandal and Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

items.title: US Senate nominee Platner halts campaign after assault allegations
items.description: A string of high-profile Democrats have withdrawn support for Platner following sexual assault allegation.

items.title: FIFA World Cup: Refereeing chief denies bias claims in Argentina-Egypt game
items.description: Pierluigi Collina said that such allegations of bias could provoke threats against referees and their families.

items.title: Albanian PM defends €4 million Kanye West concert
items.description: Albania doubled down on hosting US rapper Kanye ⁠West, as several European countries have banned his performance. PM Edi Rama claimed the gig would generate €100 million in revenue for the country.

items.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes on Iran and Tehran fires back at Gulf Arab states
items.description: The United States launched new airstrikes against Iran early Thursday, and Tehran responded by targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar in crossfire that again threatened an interim deal intended to help end the war.

items.title: Three NT corrections officers stood down with pay over prisoner escape
items.description: Three Northern Territory corrections officers have been stood down with pay over a prisoner's alleged escape during a hospital transfer, which sparked a more than 48-hour manhunt earlier this week.
